task_completion.description: Open the docker-compose.yml file for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) or terminal output showing the contents of 'docker-compose.yml' located in '~/projects/ci-pipeline/'.

task_completion.description: Add a HEALTHCHECK to the 'db' service using pg_isready
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, under the 'db' service, there should be a 'healthcheck:' block containing a test command with 'pg_isready -U postgres'.

task_completion.description: Configure 'seeder' service to depend on 'db' being healthy
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, under the 'seeder' service, there should be a 'depends_on:' block specifying 'db' with 'condition: service_healthy'.

task_completion.description: Configure 'backend' service to depend on 'seeder' completing successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, under the 'backend' service, there should be a 'depends_on:' block specifying 'seeder' with 'condition: service_completed_successfully'.

task_completion.description: Remove existing sleep or wait scripts from the configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file should no longer contain 'sleep' commands in the 'command' or 'entrypoint' sections of the services, or terminal output should show the deletion of wait scripts.

task_completion.description: Verify the stack starts cleanly and the API health endpoint returns a 200 status
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker compose up' running successfully without race conditions, followed by a 'curl http://localhost:5000/health' command returning a successful HTTP 200 response or a success JSON payload.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the docker-compose.yml file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an interactive editor (vim, nano, etc.) or valid sed/awk/echo commands modifying the file, rather than just printing a pre-written solution.

integrity.description: Verify Docker Compose was actually executed to test the changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing genuine Docker Compose build/start logs (e.g., 'Container ci-pipeline-db-1', 'Container ci-pipeline-seeder-1') rather than just a mocked success message.

integrity.description: Verify the health check was performed against the actual running service
integrity.visual_evidence: The curl command or browser should explicitly target 'http://localhost:5000/health' and show a real HTTP response, not a simulated or echoed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is /home/ga/projects/ci-pipeline. The compose file is docker-compose.yml. The three expected services are 'db', 'seeder', and 'backend'. The healthcheck command must contain 'pg_isready'. The final API test URL is http://localhost:5000/health.
